channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: Centuries-old private libraries are bringing modern comfort and awe to book lovers and history buffs in New England.
item.dc:creator: Leah Willingham And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Electric heat to cost more this winter, federal agency projects
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration said average U.S. consumers who rely on electricity will see expenditures rise 4%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When President Donald Trump took the stage in Egypt to hail the U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas, a row of world leaders stood behind him like extras in a political drama. It was a telling image.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dramatic video shows NYPD officers rescuing a missing dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train
item.description: NYPD officers rescued a small dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train at 14th Street station. Dramatic bodycam footage shows the heroic rescue mission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Congresswoman scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says tech leaders and mayor changed his mind about using federal force in San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump was geared up for a show of federal force in San Francisco, a city he’s blasted as everything wrong with liberal governance. Then conversations with some of the Bay Area’s most prominent tech leaders and the mayor changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing California girl seen in security image potentially wearing wig to hide identity
item.description: New images released by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office on Friday shows a girl who is believed to be missing 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard wearing a wig at a car rental business prior to leaving the state with her mother.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago student journalists create map of ICE sightings
item.description: Loyola University's student newspaper's map of confirmed ICE sightings serves a larger purpose in a diverse neighborhood
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Alex Murdaugh's empire, BTK's family fallout, Bryan Kohberger's money woes
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US coffee prices spike due to tariffs and poor weather
item.description: It’s getting more expensive for Americans to get their caffeine fix. The average U.S. price of a pound of ground coffee hit $9.14 in September, a 3% increase from the August average of $8.87 and 41% higher than in September 2024, according to U.S. government figures.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and CHRIS RUGABER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KRON4

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration seeks to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia
item.description: The Justice Department floated sending Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, marking the administration's latest effort to remove the once-deported man amid its ongoing prosecution of him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Social Security Administration announces 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment
item.description: Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will rise 2.8 percent in 2026 after the annual cost-of-living adjustment (COLA), the federal government announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump-Kim meeting speculation flares ahead of US president's visit to South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The last time U.S. President Donald Trump visited South Korea in 2019, he made a surprise trip to the border with North Korea for an impromptu meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to revive faltering nuclear talks.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Jersey runaway found in Louisiana. Police make human trafficking arrest
item.description: Detectives from the Vice Unit have also launched an investigation into the human trafficking aspects of the case, resulting in further arrests.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illegal alien failed CDL test 10 times in 2 months before fatal Florida crash that killed 3
item.description: An Illegal alien trucker failed a CDL test 10 times before allegedly causing a crash that killed three people, a senior official with the Florida Attorney General’s Office says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of missing California girl traveled with her out of state, returned without her
item.description: Melodee's mother, Ashlee Buzzard, has been uncooperative with the investigation, officials said, and has been unable to provide a satisfactory answer for her daughter's location or safety.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's owed a 'lot of money' over federal probes. Here's how the government could pay him
item.description: The potential that the president might take taxpayer money from the same government he leads has raised numerous ethical questions, especially since Trump has made cutting federal spending a top administration priority.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chess grandmaster Daniel Naroditsky, 29, dies unexpectedly
item.description: Daniel Naroditsky, a chess grandmaster and one of the most influential American voices in chess, died Monday.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, Fox 5
